rationale: The previous attempt failed on `assert(post1_netc.ok() <==> post2_netc.ok())` and on `assert(det_send_packet_equal(...))` because both are irreducibly unprovable from the ensures of `send_packet`: nothing forces `ok1 == ok2` (the ensures only states `post_i.ok() <==> ok_i` for two independent opaque states `post_i.state()`), and `det_send_packet_equal` further requires `post1_netc.view() == post2_netc.view()` — but `NetClient::view` projects the concrete fields `self.history@: Ghost<History>` and `self.end_point: EndPoint`, while the ensures only pins the unrelated `#[verifier::external_body] closed spec fn history(): Seq<NetEvent>` and `my_end_point(): AbstractEndPoint`, with no axiom linking those uninterpreted functions to the underlying fields.  No sound functional-uniqueness lemma exists (a witness with ok1=true,ne1=Some(e),post1 and another with ok2=false,ne2=None,post2 jointly satisfy the ensures for the same `(pre_netc, cpacket)`), so this attempt drops the unsound asserts and instead pins every truly derivable equality — transitive `my_end_point()`, per-run `history()` deltas, `send_log_entry_reflects_packet` consequences (`is Send`, `cpacket@ == abstractify_net_packet_to_sht_packet(...)` matching), and `is_marshalable_data` — making the residual gap z3-diagnosable rather than a trigger-instantiation failure.
